judge_reason: The two descriptions provide contradictory text for the same stamp: one reads 'March 29, 1948' and the other reads 'MAR 26 1942'.
original_image_tags: The image is a scan of the reverse side of a document. The paper is aged and yellowed, with two punch holes at the top and a torn corner at the top right. The typed text from the obverse side is visible in reverse (bleed-through), making it largely illegible. The only clear, non-reversed markings are official stamps and a handwritten note at the bottom.
A red rectangular stamp from the FBI's Communications Section, indicating the document was mailed on March 29, 1948.
A blue circular or oval date-stamp from the FBI Mail Room, indicating receipt on March 25, 1948, at 6:41 PM.
A handwritten filing or reference code in the bottom margin.
A handwritten note in pencil or light ink, likely an internal processing comment.
Faint, reversed text from the front of the page, indicating the content is a memo dated July 11, 1948, referencing testimony before a Senate committee, and marked "SECRET".
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: A faded scan of a single typed document page on yellowed paper, with two binder holes at the top, a red rectangular stamp near the lower right, and a slanted blue handwritten notation/stamp reading “B 1” and “SITE” near the bottom center-right.*
